behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names StdioOrFd, Fd(i32), ChildProcessStdio, pub stdout/stderr, extra_stdio: Vec<StdioOrFd>, libc::dup(), from_raw_fd, borrow::<ChildProcessStdio>, use deno_io::ChildProcessStdio, as_i64(), error strings, string variants, ChildStdio fields, and FileResource removal — all assertions in tests match these specifics.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md essentially dictates the implementation: 'rename StdioOrRid to StdioOrFd', 'replaced by Fd(i32)', 'use as_i64()', 'duplicate with libc::dup()', 'wrap via from_raw_fd', 'state.borrow::<ChildProcessStdio>()', exact error strings — leaving almost no reasoning about WHAT to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on specific identifier names (StdioOrFd, Fd(i32), ChildProcessStdio, as_i64, from_raw_fd, libc::dup) — an alternative correct fix using different names (e.g., RawFd, dup2, a different borrow pattern) would fail despite correct behavior.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While many f2p tests grep source text, the suite also runs cargo check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test --lib, and cargo fmt which execute and verify behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Stub implementations cannot satisfy the cargo check/clippy/test commands plus the text assertions simultaneously; the combination requires the actual refactor.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each of the 6 expected-behavior items from instruction.md has corresponding test coverage (enum rename, i32 variant, dup/from_raw_fd, ChildProcessStdio struct, extra_stdio typing, inherit borrow, preserved string variants, ChildStdio fields).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with header comments distinguishing fail_to_pass, pass_to_pass static, and pass_to_pass repo_tests sections.

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data output; this is a Rust refactor task.

no_typos.reason: Filenames (ext/process/lib.rs, ext/io/lib.rs) and identifiers are consistent between instruction.md and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names exact identifiers and code constructs to introduce (StdioOrFd, Fd(i32), libc::dup(), from_raw_fd, state.borrow::<ChildProcessStdio>(), as_i64()) — this goes beyond domain hints to prescribing implementation.
